en.text: O best of the Bharat dynasty
O descendant of the Bharat dynasty
That knowledge of kṣetra and kṣetrajña
the quality of desirelessness for honour
of the state of being an enjoyer or a sufferer
the way of liberation of the living beings from material nature
the state of Brahma is reached
and by the aphorisms of Vedānta
the ten senses (the five senses of action—voice, hands, legs, anus, and genital; and the five senses of perception – eye, tongue, skin, nose, and ear)
(He is) the Lord of all qualities
Attachment to the modes (as body, senses, etc.)
and the transformations of the modes of nature, as happiness, unhappiness, sorrow, and delusion, etc
detachment from the objects of the senses
corresponding objects of the senses (form, taste, touch, smell, and sound)
of birth, death, infirmity, and disease
and the object of knowledge (the Lord in His aspects of Brahma, Paramātmā, and Bhagavān)
via the presiding demigods of the senses, of the production of the effect, as the body, and of the cause, as the senses
as kṣetrajña, the knower of the field
and the knower of the field
as kṣetrajña, the knower of the field
as born of the combination of the field and its knower
between the field and its knower
and beyond non-being, beyond the material cause
nor is affected by any action
beyond being, beyond the material effect
is not tainted by anything, is untouched
(Although) transcendental to the modes of nature
and being transcendental to the material modes
Situated within (the body which is an effect of) material nature
Material nature (with the presence of puruṣ)
and the jīvātma, individual soul, described as ‘knower of the field’
(He) illuminates all the senses and their functions
births in higher and lower species
and the vision of the goal of true knowledge
the transformations – body, senses, and their functions
when their faith is deepened by hearing

ru.text: согласно взгляду на мироздание, явленному мудрецами прошлого
гун̣ы, состояния или качества материальной материи
